skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit images on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — a smart router across the full image-model catalog: FLUX 2 (Klein 9B/4B, Pro, Dev, Flash, Turbo, Max), Google Nano Banana 2 / Pro, OpenAI GPT Image 2, ByteDance Seedream 5 / 4-5 / 4-0 and Dreamina 4-0, Alibaba Qwen Image and Z-Image Turbo, Wan 2-7. Covers both text-to-image (t2i) and image-to-image / edit (i2i) endpoints — the skill picks the right model for the user's actual intent (typography precision, photoreal portraits, sub-second iteration, multi-reference brand styling, open-weights workflow) and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke. Triggers on "generate image", "make a picture", "text to image", "AI image", "make an image of …", "image to image", "i2i", or any explicit ask to create or restyle an image.
skill.summary: Generate and edit images with 11+ AI models via the RunComfy CLI
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 309 potential issues, primarily false positives triggered by documentation patterns. All external command flags (213) are bash command examples in documentation, not executable code. Network flags (63) are legitimate runcomfy.com URLs. Filesystem flags (5) are URL placeholder patterns. The skill properly mitigates shell injection risk by passing prompts as JSON via --input flag. Risk level is medium due to inherent external command usage, but skill is safe to publish with proper documentation.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Pattern in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 213 instances of shell command patterns (runcomfy run invocations) which triggered static analysis. These are documentation examples, not executable code. The skill explicitly declares allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *) and limits execution to the runcomfy CLI only. Shell injection is mitigated by passing prompts as JSON via --input flag.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 213 external command flags are bash command examples in SKILL.md documentation, not actual code execution. The skill's Security section explicitly states 'The skill never instructs the agent to run anything other than runcomfy <subcommand>' and mitigates shell injection by passing prompts as JSON.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: 63 hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com and docs.runcomfy.com are legitimate documentation links, not exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to runcomfy.com domains for documentation, model catalog, and CLI reference. No evidence of data exfiltration or unauthorized endpoint communication.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Pattern in URL Placeholders
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged URL placeholder patterns like 'https://.../portrait.jpg' as path traversal. These are example documentation URLs, not actual file paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The '...' in example URLs is a placeholder notation, not an actual path traversal attempt. Real URLs follow the pattern 'https://domain.com/image.jpg'.

content.user_title: Generate AI images with 11+ models via RunComfy CLI
content.value_statement: Creating images for marketing campaigns, product mockups, or creative projects requires selecting the right AI model. This skill routes your request to the optimal model (FLUX, GPT Image 2, Seedream, Nano Banana) and provides the exact CLI command with documented prompting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 11+ AI models (FLUX 2, GPT Image 2, Nano Banana 2, Seedream 5, Qwen Image, Wan 2-7, Z-Image Turbo, Dreamina)
Edit existing images with identity-preserving image-to-image transformations
Select the optimal model based on use case: typography precision, photoreal portraits, speed iteration, or open-weights workflows
Create multilingual image content with accurate in-image text rendering (Japanese kana, Cyrillic, Arabic)
Generate batch variants for ideation with multiple seeds or reference images
Apply brand styling using multi-reference conditioning for consistent visual identity
limitations: Requires RunComfy account and API token for authentication
Generated images depend on AI model capabilities; results vary by model and prompt quality
Some models (FLUX 2 Pro, Nano Banana Pro) have higher cost than faster alternatives
Mask-driven inpainting requires the separate image-edit skill

use_cases.title: Marketing Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Create brand-consistent campaign visuals with exact headlines, product shots, and multilingual variations using GPT Image 2 for typography and Seedream 5 for photoreal content.

use_cases.title: Product Concept Ideation
use_cases.description: Rapidly generate and iterate product mockups using Nano Banana 2 for fast 4-up batch ideation at low resolution, then refine with FLUX 2 Klein 9B for final delivery.

use_cases.title: Creative Concept Art
use_cases.description: Produce stylized illustrations and concept art using Dreamina 4-0 or FLUX 2, with open-weights support via Qwen Image or Wan 2-7 for LoRA-customized workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your subject]. Use [optional: style direction like 'photoreal', 'illustration', 'cinematic lighting'].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Portrait with Specific Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [lens type, e.g., '85mm portrait'] of [subject description] by [location/setting], with [lighting direction, e.g., 'soft natural light from a window']. Photoreal style.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need photorealistic portraits or product shots

prompt_templates.title: Brand Campaign with Typography
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a [format, e.g., 'minimal product poster'] with a centered bold headline that reads exactly '[your headline text]' in [font style] on a [background description]. Below the headline, [secondary text description].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need exact text rendered in images for campaigns

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Image Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the uploaded image as reference, keep [elements to preserve] unchanged. Change [specific elements to modify] to [new description].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to edit an existing image while maintaining certain aspects

output_examples.input: Generate a photorealistic portrait of a woman by a window with soft natural light
output_examples.output: A high-quality portrait image generated using Seedream 5 Lite or FLUX 2 Klein 9B, featuring a woman posed near a window with natural window light creating soft shadows and a shallow depth of field effect.

output_examples.input: Create a product poster with the headline 'SUMMER 2026' in bold white sans-serif on a navy background
output_examples.output: A landscape-format poster generated using GPT Image 2, with the exact headline 'SUMMER 2026' rendered in bold white sans-serif typography, centered on a deep navy background with clean product presentation.

best_practices: Lead with the subject, describe the scene second, and add modifiers last for best results. Example: 'A small purple cat on a moss stone, golden hour lighting' instead of 'Generate a creative shot of a cat'.
Use GPT Image 2 when exact in-image text is required. Always quote text exactly with quotation marks and name the script for non-Latin languages (e.g., 'Japanese kana').
Start ideation with faster models (Nano Banana 2 at 0.5K resolution) and upgrade to higher-quality models (FLUX 2 Klein 9B at 2K) only for final delivery to manage costs.
anti_patterns: Avoid generic prompts like 'make it look good' or 'create something beautiful'. Be specific about subject, style, lighting, and composition for consistent results.
Do not use multiple complex instructions in a single prompt. For compound edits, make separate passes and iterate rather than asking for multiple changes at once.
Avoid cranking steps past 28 on FLUX models. Diminishing returns occur beyond this point, and 4-8 steps are sufficient for initial ideation rounds.

faq.question: Which model should I use for in-image text?
faq.answer: Use GPT Image 2 for best-in-class text rendering. Quote text exactly with quotation marks and specify the script for non-Latin languages.

faq.question: How do I get photorealistic portraits?
faq.answer: Use Seedream 5 Lite for best skin tones and natural lighting, or FLUX 2 Klein 9B with explicit lens and lighting language at 25 steps.

faq.question: How can I reduce costs during ideation?
faq.answer: Use Nano Banana 2 at 0.5K resolution with 4-up batch generation. Upgrade to higher resolution only for final deliverables.

faq.question: Can I edit specific regions of an image?
faq.answer: This skill handles one-shot edits. For mask-driven inpainting or controlled outpainting, use the separate image-edit skill.

faq.question: How do I maintain consistent identity across batches?
faq.answer: Use the same model family (e.g., Seedream 5 Lite t2i followed by Seedream 5 Lite edit) and reuse seed values for reproducible results.

faq.question: What authentication is required?
faq.answer: Run 'runcomfy login' to authenticate interactively, or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments.
